items.hook: French president Jacques Chirac escapes an assassination attempt from Maxime Brunerie during a Bastille Day parade at Champs-Élysées.
items.body: Jacques René Chirac was a French politician who was President of France from 1995 to 2007. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Mariner 4 flyby of Mars takes the first close-up photos of another planet. The photographs take approximately six hours to be transmitted back to Earth.
items.body: Mariner 4 was the fourth in a series of spacecraft intended for planetary exploration in a flyby mode. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Sam Neill’s final interview: ‘I’d like to think that, in life, I’m a goodie’
items.body: The actor, who has died aged 78, recently answered

items.hook: A man ploughs a truck into a Bastille Day celebration in Nice, France, killing 86 people and injuring another 434 before being shot by police.
items.body: On the evening of 14 July 2016, a 19-tonne cargo truck was deliberately driven into crowds of people celebrating Bastille Day on the Promenade des Anglais in Nice, France, resulting in the deaths of 86 people and injuring 458 others. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Peruvian explorer and farmer Agustín Lizárraga discovers Machu Picchu, the "Lost City of the Incas".
items.body: Agustín Lizárraga Ruiz was a Peruvian explorer and farmer who visited the Inca site of Machu Picchu on 14 July 1902, nine years before American explorer Hiram Bingham brought it to international attention. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Dana Goodyear is an American journalist and poet, the author of the forthcoming book Anything That Moves, and the co-founder of Figment, an on-line literary community.
items.body: She is a staff writer at The New Yorker and teaches in the Master of Professional Writing program at the University of Southern California. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Rus Flight 9633 crashes during takeoff from Chkalovsky Airport, killing all 10 people on board.
items.body: Rus Flight 9633 refers to the crash involving an IL-76TD aircraft operated by Rus Airlines from Chkalovsky Airport to Taiyuan Wusu Airport with intermediate stops at Alykel Airport and Bratsk Airport. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Invite review – A-list ensemble electrify hilarious couples night gone wrong comedy

items.body:
Cinephiles are crossing oceans to see the film at its highest possible resolution – and only 41 cinemas in the world are equipped to do it

items.hook: Bodyguard Kiba 2 , also known as Bodyguard Kiba 2, is a 1973 Japanese martial arts film starring Sonny Chiba.
items.body: It is the sequel to the earlier 1973 film Bodyguard Kiba based on an action manga by Ikki Kajiwara. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: ‘A frightening piece to perform’: can Yoko Ono’s Cut Piece still shock?
items.body:
Sixty years after its first staging, performance artist MPA is restaging the provocative piece in Los Angeles

Yoko Ono: Music of the Mind, a traveli

items.hook: Udonis Johneal Haslem is an American professional basketball executive and former player.
items.body: He is the vice president of basketball development for the Miami Heat, where he spent his entire 20-year playing career in the National Basketball Association (NBA). Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Al-Wajiz fi Fiqh al-Imam al-Shafi'i or The Condensed in Imam Shafi’i’s Jurisprudence is a concise summary of Shafi’i Fiqh and 'Ilm al-Khilaf written by Imam al-Ghazali the leading juristconsult of his time.
items.body: It mentions the contrary opinions of Al-Shafi'i including Abu Hanifa, Malik Ibn Anas and Al-Muzani. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘He made wine and he shared it. What more do you want?’: Sam Neill remembered by his co-stars
items.body: The actors Lindsay Duncan and Charles Dance, alongside director Peter Webber, pay tribute to a practical joker, unpretentious craftsman and ‘very cool guy’

items.hook: Mario Bros. is released in Japan, beginning the popular Super Mario Bros franchise.
items.body: Mario Bros. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Andrew Lloyd Webber says Broadway in ‘dire danger’ as Cats musical announces early closing
items.body:
The legendary composer warned theaters could soon meet the same fate as Hollywood’s ‘empty soundstages’

Andrew Lloyd Webber has spoken out about the precarious s

items.hook: Norfolk & Dedham, and member of the N&D|Union Mutual Insurance Group, is a mutual insurance carrier based in Dedham, Massachusetts, comprising three regional property and casualty insurance companies which market personal and commercial insurance product lines through independent insurance agents.
items.body: The group conducts business in Massachusetts, New Hampshire, and New Jersey, and writes over $400 million in Direct insurance premium. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: I interviewed Sam Neill in 2024. He was even more charming than I’d expected | Zoe Williams
items.body: The actor, who has died aged 78, was refreshingly candid about Hollywood, filmmaking, his cancer treatment – and why he preferred life on his New Zealand farm to the movie business
